LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT CONTINUED FROM JUN-15-2012 TO JUN-28-2012, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 PER STIPULATION OF COUNSEL.
ORDER ON DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT (OVERRULED)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT STEPHENS & STEPHENS XII LLC. AND STEPHENS & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE COMPLAINT AND JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE DOCUMENTS DO NOT SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS NECESSARILY TIME-BARRED. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER ANY ISSUES CONCERNING THE JURY VERDICT AND JUDGE KARNOW'S POST-JUDGMENT ORDERS IN THE OTHER CASE BECAUSE THOSE MATTERS WERE NOT RAISED IN THE MOVING PAPERS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-14-2012 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2012 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-13-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-19-2012 Continued To Sep-12-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-28-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nina G. Shapirshteyn, Esq., of O'Reilly Collins, Tel. 650.358.5913, appearing Defendants and moving party.
Reginald D. Steer, Esq., of Akin Gump Strauss Hauer & Feld, Tel. 415.765.9520, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STEPHENS & STEPHENS XII LLC. AND STEPHENS & COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled. The complaint and judicially noticeable documents do not show that plaintiff's claim is necessarily time-barred. The court did not consider any issues concerning the jury verdict and Judge Karnow's post-judgment orders in the other case because those matters were not raised in the moving papers. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
